title: pittosporum revolutum var. tomentosum
intro: Pittosporum revolutum var. tomentosum is a variety of the Pittosporum revolutum plant, commonly known as the "New Zealand tobacco tree" or "Toothbrush tree", due to its distinctive, toothbrush-like flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum revolutum var. tomentosum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate sore throats. Pittosporum revolutum var. tomentosum can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote respiratory health.
